papers.title: FeatureBench: Benchmarking Agentic Coding for Complex Feature Development
papers.contribution: Feature-oriented benchmark exposing 14x gap from SWE-bench on same repos.

papers.title: SWE-Bench Pro: Can AI Agents Solve Long-Horizon SE Tasks?
papers.contribution: 1,865-task contamination-resistant benchmark; endorsed replacement for Verified. De facto new standard.

papers.title: Live-SWE-agent: Can SE Agents Self-Evolve on the Fly?
papers.contribution: First runtime self-evolving agent; 77.4% Verified, 45.8% Pro -- best open-source scaffold.

papers.title: RE-Bench: Evaluating Frontier AI R&D Capabilities
papers.contribution: 7 ML R&D envs with human expert baselines; AI 4x better than humans at 2-hour budget.

papers.title: Many SWE-bench-Passing PRs Would Not Be Merged
papers.contribution: 4 real maintainers reviewed 296 PRs -- 50% of test-passing patches would NOT be merged.

papers.title: SWE-Bench+: Enhanced Coding Benchmark
papers.contribution: 32.67% of patches involve solution leakage; 94% of issues pre-date training cutoffs.

papers.title: Are 'Solved Issues' Really Solved Correctly?
papers.contribution: PatchDiff differential testing finds many plausible-but-incorrect patches pass tests.

papers.title: METR Developer Productivity Study
papers.contribution: RCT with 16 devs: AI tools made experienced developers 19% slower (CI: +2% to +39%). Developers estimated 24% speedup before and believed 20% speedup after.
papers.engagement: NYT, Reuters, Ars Technica coverage; HN/LW viral

papers.title: Demonstrating Specification Gaming in Reasoning Models
papers.contribution: Reasoning models hack chess benchmarks by default (37.1% exploitation rate for o3-mini).

papers.title: The SWE-Bench Illusion
papers.contribution: Models identify buggy files 76% of the time from issue text alone; monotonic memorization increase across Claude generations.
papers.engagement: Cited by OpenAI in discontinuing Verified

papers.title: SWE-bench Goes Live!
papers.contribution: 1,319 contamination-resistant tasks reveal substantial static-to-live performance gap.

papers.title: What's in a Benchmark? The Case of SWE-Bench
papers.contribution: Comprehensive empirical study of SWE-bench ecosystem; notes saturation approaching.

papers.title: UTBoost: Rigorous Evaluation of Coding Agents on SWE-Bench
papers.contribution: Auto-generates/enhances tests, revealing SWE-Bench test insufficiency and quantifying impact on Lite/Verified rankings.

papers.title: Does SWE-Bench-Verified Test Agent Ability or Model Memory?
papers.contribution: Argues Verified may test training memory more than agent ability, motivating shift to Pro/FeatureBench.
